steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the scale shortcut: find the linear ratio
steps.content:
Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, triangle $SKL \sim$ triangle $STR$ (AA: shared angle $S$, and $\angle LKS = \angle RTS = 90^{\circ}$). The linear scale factor is:
$$\frac{LK}{RT} = \frac{22}{66} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Recover ST from the area
steps.content:
Area of right triangle $RST = \frac{1}{2} \cdot RT \cdot ST$:
$$528 = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 66 \cdot ST \implies ST = 16$$

steps.title: Apply scale factor to find SK, then KT
steps.content:
Corresponding sides scale by $\frac{1}{3}$, so $SK = \frac{1}{3} \cdot ST = \frac{1}{3} \cdot 16 = \frac{16}{3}$.
$$KT = ST - SK = 16 - \frac{16}{3} = \frac{48 - 16}{3} = \frac{32}{3} \approx 10.667$$
The answer is 32/3, 10.666, or 10.667.
